identity.short_name: Conditional probability a contract succeeds unless West holds both Q♠ and J♠

subdomain(s): conditional probability on card deals (hypergeometric model)
events defined by key-card ownership patterns

problem_spec.summary: Given a fixed 26-card defender pool in a bridge deal and an ownership constraint from trick 1, assume a uniform conditional distribution of the remaining cards between East and West; compute the probability a contract succeeds when failure occurs exactly if West holds both specified key cards.
givens: Standard 52-card deck; four players with North-South fixed and known.
The defenders (East and West) jointly hold a known 26-card set D; all other cards belong to North-South.
Trick-1 information: West is known to hold a specific card (here ♥Q).

Model: among the remaining unknown cards, all splits between East (13 cards total) and West (13 cards total) consistent with the ownership constraint are equally likely (uniform conditional deal model).
The contract fails iff West holds both specified key cards (here ♠Q and ♠J); otherwise it succeeds.
ask: Let X be the conditional probability the contract succeeds. Write X=A/B in lowest terms. Find B-A.

parameter(s).name: fixed_holdings
parameter(s).purpose: defines the conditioned sample space (defenders' card pool)

parameter(s).name: success_event
parameter(s).purpose: defines success via an ownership pattern of two key cards (here {♠Q,♠J})

parameter(s).name: output_transform
parameter(s).purpose: map probability X to the requested integer B-A

invariant(s).name: uniform_conditional_deal_model
invariant(s).statement: All defender deals consistent with the constraints are equally likely; equivalently, West's remaining cards form a uniformly random subset of the unknown cards of the required size.

invariant(s).name: event_depends_only_on_west_subset
invariant(s).statement: The success/failure event depends only on whether West's subset contains both key cards, so probabilities reduce to hypergeometric counting.

essential_assumption(s): The only information extracted from trick 1 is the ownership constraint on the specified card; no further strategic inference is used.
Conditioned on the constraint, all consistent allocations of the remaining cards are equally likely.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The conditional sample space is finite and uniform, so X is uniquely defined as a rational favorable/total ratio; reducing X to lowest terms makes B-A uniquely determined.

technique(s).name: define_conditional_sample_space
inputs: defender pool D and ownership constraint(s)
technique(s).purpose: formalize the uniform conditional distribution as a finite set of equally likely deals
outputs: total count |Ω| as a binomial coefficient

technique(s).name: compute_probability
inputs: event described by containment of key cards in West's subset

technique(s).name: fraction_reduction
inputs: rational X as a quotient of binomial coefficients
technique(s).purpose: reduce to lowest terms and compute B-A

theorem(s).name: Basic conditional probability on uniform finite spaces
Hypergeometric counting for fixed-size subsets
theorem(s).role: count deals where West's subset contains specified cards

theorem(s).name: Uniqueness of reduced fractions
theorem(s).role: ensures B-A is well-defined from X

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Uniform probability on constrained combinatorial configurations

critical_trick(s): Encode the deal (given the constraint) as a uniformly random subset selection for West.
Count unfavorable outcomes by forcing the two key cards into West's subset and choosing the remainder freely.
Simplify the resulting ratio of binomial coefficients to a reduced fraction.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: exact binomial coefficients via math.comb for verification

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: optional brute-force enumeration over subsets for small sanity-check variants (not required here)

task(s).name: compute_B_minus_A
task(s).reason: extract A,B from reduced Fraction and output B-A
